mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high PAYEMS periods fall below the 30th percentile of historical returns, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 3.5% signals tight labor market conditions that constrain wage growth and inflation, creating a favorable environment for equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods when UNRATE < 3.5% are negative or below the median historical return for such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and core inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal a dovish monetary policy stance that supports equity risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to explain more than 10% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or the combined signal produces negative returns in 60%+ of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate earnings visibility, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in INDPRO contraction periods exceed the 70th percentile of historical returns, or the predictive power of INDPRO declines below 0.15 in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly expansion in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong economic growth that supports corporate profits and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in GDP growth periods fall below the 40th percentile of historical returns, or the correlation between GDPC1 and SPX returns drops below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly surge in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.5 million units signals strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in HOUST surge periods are negative or below the 25th percentile of historical returns for such events.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer credit (RSAFS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signal strong household financial health and labor market conditions that drive equity market optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and UNRATE fails to explain more than 15% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or the combined signal produces negative returns in 50%+ of triggered instances.
